headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the most negative reaction on limited evidence after a commenter described a traumatic false medical diagnosis from ChatGPT, while Gemini was criticized and Copilot got a positive note [E1][E2][E3].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was viewed negatively on limited evidence after a commenter said ChatGPT gave a terrifying but incorrect medical diagnosis and would not back off even after follow-up [E1].

Google Gemini was discussed negatively on limited evidence, with one commenter arguing Gemini 3 Flash was slower, less accurate, more expensive, and less flexible than alternatives while attracting undue praise [E2].
Microsoft Copilot came across positively on limited evidence, as one commenter said they were pretty happy with it in VS Code and found the edit-accept workflow useful [E3].

evidence.excerpt: A year or so ago, I fed my wife's blood work results into chatgpt and it came back with a terrifying diagnosis. Even after a lot of back and forth it stuck to its guns. We went to a specialist who performed some additional tests and explained that the condition cannot be diagnosed with just the original blood work and said that she did not have the condition. The whole thing was a borderline traumatic ordeal that I'm still pretty pissed about.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names ChatGPT and describes a false, alarming medical assessment that persisted despite follow-up, expressing severe dissatisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Faster, more accurate, less expensive, more flexible in terms of hosting and available months before Gemini 3 Flash, I really struggle to understand why Flash got such positive attention at launch.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly mentions Gemini 3 Flash and criticizes the praise it received by unfavorably comparing it on speed, accuracy, price, flexibility, and timing.

evidence.excerpt: I'm pretty happy with Copilot in VS Code. Type what change I want Claude to make in the Copilot panel, and then use the VS Code in context diffs to accept or reject the proposed changes.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says they are 'pretty happy' with Copilot in VS Code and describes a productive workflow using it.
